_README: Deny-by-default policy for the defender runtime gate (runtime/permission.py).
Issue #379: the read-only Bash lane runs shell=False (runtime/bash_exec.py), so
the gate no longer parses a shell string and predicts bash — it validates the
SAME argv-stage decomposition the executor runs. With the shell gone there are
no per-argument constraints to express here: the allowlist is just program
names. `find` was dropped (template discovery is Read/Grep now), which removed
the one tool that needed arg-level deny rules.
viewers  = read-only utilities allowed in any composition, both agents.
The non-adapter defender-* shims are added from the taxonomy
(hooks/_cmd_segments.NON_ADAPTER_SHIMS), the single source of truth
for the adapter/non-adapter split, so they are NOT re-listed here.
adapters = whether the agent may invoke a data-source adapter (defender-<sys>
or scripts/adapters/<sys>_cli.py). main: no (dispatch gather);
gather: yes, captured transparently.
raw_reads = whether the agent may read / jq gather_raw/**. main: no (consumes
the gather summary); gather: yes (verify its own query result). A
learning-loop agent (the judge) declares this in its own AgentPolicy.
adapter_sql_pipe = whether `defender-<sys> ... --raw | defender-sql '<SQL>'` is
sanctioned (gather only): the adapter stage is captured, then the
payload is aggregated through the sandboxed defender-sql.
read_deny = secrets / ground-truth the agent may never read. Kept as a
declarative last line (defense-in-depth) even though OS isolation is
the eventual boundary. substrings match the filename; dirs match any
path component.
